name: Dillard's Mastercard®

verdict.text: Thin rewards and high APR — only for Dillard's regulars who pay in full

earn_rates.category: All other purchases where Mastercard is accepted

earn_note: This is the open-loop Mastercard version of Dillard's card — it earns outside Dillard's, unlike the closed-loop, store-only Dillard's Credit Card, which earns the same 2 pts/$1 at Dillard's but nothing anywhere else. Citi acquired Dillard's card portfolio (private-label and co-branded) from Wells Fargo in January 2024; both card products are now issued by Citibank, N.A. New cardholders get a 10%-off shopping pass (up to $100) after $100 spent on the day of approval.

redemption_options.method: Every 1,500 points converts to either a 10% Off Shopping Pass or a $10 Reward Certificate, redeemable at Dillard's/dillards.com

points.per_100k: About $667–$1,000 in Dillard's rewards, depending on whether points are redeemed as $10 certificates or 10%-off passes
points.note: Rewards redeem only at Dillard's — no cash, statement credit, or third-party gift cards. At 1,500 points = $10, the 2 pts/$1 rate at Dillard's/gas/groceries works out to roughly 1.3% back, and the 1 pt/$1 base rate to about 0.67% — both well below a typical flat-rate cash-back card.

transfer_partners.highlight: No transfer partners — Dillard's Rewards points redeem only at Dillard's.

insurance.detail: Not confirmed on Citi's or Dillard's public product pages for this card
Not confirmed on Citi's or Dillard's public product pages for this card

protection_note: A standard (not World Elite) Mastercard tier with no confirmed purchase protection, extended warranty, or travel insurance beyond baseline Mastercard ID Theft Protection and Zero Liability Protection referenced in third-party reviews. Treat this as a rewards-and-financing card with no meaningful insurance suite.
rental_note: No rental car coverage found for this card.

services.name: Special financing offers
services.detail: In-store-only (not online) promotional financing periodically offered on qualifying Dillard's purchases — check current terms before financing.

services.name: Foreign transaction fee
services.detail: 3% of the U.S. dollar amount of each purchase made outside the US, per Citi's cardholder agreement on file with the CFPB (as of mid-2026).

additional_cards.title: Authorized users

benefits.text: Earns rewards on their spend (into your account)
No dedicated insurance to share — this card doesn't carry a confirmed protection suite

additional_cards.note: Free to add, useful mainly for pooling a household's Dillard's/gas/grocery spend toward the redemption thresholds.

timeline.text: Citi purchased Dillard's private-label and co-branded credit card portfolios from Wells Fargo, becoming the new issuer of both the closed-loop Dillard's Credit Card and this open-loop Dillard's Mastercard.

Dillard's/Citi issue two distinct card products: the closed-loop, store-only Dillard's Credit Card (2 pts/$1 at Dillard's, unusable elsewhere) and this open-loop Dillard's Mastercard (same 2 pts/$1 at Dillard's/gas/groceries, plus 1 pt/$1 everywhere else Mastercard is accepted). Confirm which one you're comparing — they are not the same card.
